topic: A person finds out they were adopted through a DNA test
raw_output:
[story 1]

The crisp white paper lay on the kitchen counter, the stark lines of the DNA report mocking the messy reality of my life. My thumb traced the unfamiliar name printed beneath the section detailing my biological origins. A heavy, cold weight settled in my chest, making each breath shallow. I stared at the screen, the numbers swimming before my eyes, trying to anchor myself to something solid, but everything felt adrift. The familiar contours of my existence had just been rearranged by a biological fact I hadn't anticipated. I picked up the phone, my hand trembling slightly, the silence in the apartment amplifying the frantic drumming in my ears.

[story 2]

She sat on the edge of the sofa, the fabric cool against her skin, refusing to meet my gaze. The results were undeniable, a stark revelation delivered with clinical detachment. Every muscle in my jaw tightened, a physical manifestation of the internal struggle. I watched her, cataloging the subtle shifts in her posture—the way her shoulders hunched inward, the slight hesitation before she spoke. "So," I managed, the word coming out thin and reedy, "what does this actually *mean*?" Her eyes flickered towards the window, avoiding mine. The air in the room seemed thick, pressurized, as if the very atmosphere was resisting the truth I was trying to force into the light.

[story 3]

I walked through the park, the familiar green of the trees offering no solace. Each step felt weighted, deliberate, as if moving through deep water. The DNA confirmation had landed like a dropped stone in a still pond, sending ripples that spread outward, disturbing the surface I was desperately trying to maintain. I stopped beneath an old oak, leaning my forehead against the rough bark. A slow, shuddering exhale escaped me, tasting of dust and apprehension. The future, once a vague expanse, now felt like a maze with no discernible exit, and I had no map.
